toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Artifact Hub Tracker

## Current phase

Phase 4 — MCP Core Slice

## Done

* Design plan completed (`docs/plans/2026-05-28-artifact-hub-design.md`)
* WRITEUP.md skeleton started with draft decision notes
* `requirements.md` present

### Phase 1 — Foundation (complete)

* Next.js 16 initialized (App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, ESLint)
* Dependencies installed: `@supabase/supabase-js`, `@supabase/ssr`, `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `nanoid`
* `supabase/migrations/001_initial.sql` — full schema: enums, `artifacts`, `feedback`, `share_links`, `feedback_summaries` tables with indexes
* `src/types/index.ts` — all domain types + service result helper
* `src/lib/supabase.ts` — browser client, SSR server client, admin client
* `src/lib/storage.ts` — signed URL generation (public 1h, share-link capped at `min(1h, remaining)`) + upload helper
* `src/lib/auth.ts` — `requireMcpAuth` / `isMcpAuthorized` for `/api/mcp/*` routes
* `.env.example` + `.env.local` template (no real values committed)
* `.gitignore` protects `node_modules`, `.next`, and all `.env*.local` files
* `npm run build` passes clean (TypeScript OK, no warnings)

### Phase 2 — Core Web (complete)

* `src/lib/services/artifacts.ts` — listArtifacts, getArtifact, createArtifact
* `src/lib/services/share.ts` — createShareLink, validateShareLink (expiry checked)
* API routes: `GET/POST /api/artifacts`, `GET /api/artifacts/[id]`, `GET /api/artifacts/[id]/preview`, `POST /api/share`, `GET /api/share/[token]`
* Components: ArtifactCard, GalleryFilter (URL-based filter state), ArtifactPreview (image/PDF/sandboxed HTML), ShareButton, PublishForm
* Pages: gallery (`/`), artifact detail (`/artifacts/[id]`, 403 for unlisted), share link view (`/share/[token]`), publish (`/publish`)
* Auto-share-link created on publish for unlisted artifacts; success screen shows copy-able URL
* `npm run build` clean — all 9 routes registered

### Phase 3 — Feedback + LLM (complete)

* `src/lib/services/feedback.ts` — listFeedback, addFeedback
* `src/lib/services/summarize.ts` — getCachedSummary, getSummary (cache-first: returns cached if feedback_count unchanged; calls Gemini + upserts when missing, stale, or force_refresh=true)
* API routes: `GET/POST /api/artifacts/[id]/feedback`, `POST /api/artifacts/[id]/summarize`
* Components: FeedbackList (server, type/status badges), FeedbackForm (client, router.refresh() on submit), FeedbackSummary (client, stale badge + generate/regenerate button, digest panels)
* Detail page and share link page both updated with feedback + summary sections
* Prompt version stored as "v1"; model name stored per summary row for transparency
* `npm run build` clean — 11 routes registered

### Pre-Phase-4 hardening (complete)

TypeScript reviewer findings addressed before MCP integration:
* **Gemini JSON validation** — `getCachedSummary` now propagates real DB errors (only returns null on PGRST116); `getSummary` distinguishes artifact 404 vs 500; `response.text` checked for empty before `JSON.parse`; parsed result validated with `isValidSummaryData` guard before storing
* **Enum input validation** — `POST /api/artifacts` validates `type` and `visibility` against allowed enum values, validates `tags` shape, builds typed `CreateArtifactBody` (no more `as any`); `POST /api/artifacts/[id]/feedback` validates `feedback_type` enum and builds typed `CreateFeedbackBody`
* **Pagination guard** — `GET /api/artifacts` returns 400 on non-integer or negative `limit`/`offset` (was passing NaN to Supabase)
* **`storage_path` stripped** — removed from all public API responses: `GET/POST /api/artifacts`, `GET /api/artifacts/[id]`, `GET /api/share/[token]`; `ArtifactPublic = Omit<Artifact, "storage_path">` added to types
* **`createShareLink` existence check** — service now verifies artifact exists before insert; bad `artifact_id` returns clean 404 instead of raw FK constraint error
* **Visibility on feedback GET** — `GET /api/artifacts/[id]/feedback` now checks visibility and returns 403 for unlisted artifacts (matching `/api/artifacts/[id]` policy); POST still allows feedback from share-link holders
* Build clean — all 11 routes, TypeScript OK

## In progress

* Phase 4 — MCP Core Slice (not started yet)

## Blockers and decisions

* **LLM provider switched to Gemini** — `@anthropic-ai/sdk` removed; `@google/genai` installed. Summarization will use `GEMINI_API_KEY` + `GEMINI_MODEL` (default `gemini-2.5-flash`). Decision: avoid unnecessary Anthropic API costs; Gemini Flash is sufficient for text-only feedback summarization.
* **Manual Supabase setup required before Phase 2 can use real data:**
  1. Create a Supabase project
  2. Run `supabase/migrations/001_initial.sql` (`npx supabase db push`)
  3. Create a private `artifacts` storage bucket
  4. Fill in `.env.local` with real credentials (`GEMINI_API_KEY` instead of `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`)
* No code blockers

### Phase 4 — MCP Core Slice (complete)

API MCP routes (all require `x-api-key`, call same services as web routes, no visibility restriction):
* `GET /api/mcp/artifacts` — list with optional type/tags/visibility/limit/offset filters
* `GET /api/mcp/artifacts/[id]` — full detail: artifact + feedback list + 1-hour signed URL
* `POST /api/mcp/share` — create share link (delegates to createShareLink service)
* `POST /api/mcp/summarize` — cache-first summarize with artifact_id + force_refresh in body

MCP server (`mcp/` package — Node.js stdio, ESM):
* `mcp/src/client.ts` — HTTP helper reads `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY` + `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL`
* `mcp/src/tools/artifacts.ts` — `list_artifacts`, `get_artifact` (human-readable text output)
* `mcp/src/tools/share.ts` — `create_share_link`
* `mcp/src/tools/summarize.ts` — `summarize_feedback`
* `mcp/src/index.ts` — McpServer + StdioServerTransport entry point
* `mcp/tsconfig.json` — node16 module resolution, compiles to `mcp/dist/`
* `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL` added to `.env.example`
* `mcp/dist/` added to `.gitignore`
* Web `npm run build` clean — 15 routes (4 new MCP routes registered)
* `cd mcp && npm run build` clean — 6 JS files compiled, TypeScript OK

## In progress

* Phase 5 — MCP Extended (not started yet)

Phase 4 complete and committed. Here's what was built:

**4 API MCP routes** (`/api/mcp/*`) — auth-gated, visibility bypass:
- `GET /api/mcp/artifacts` — list with full filter support including `visibility` (unlike the web route which is always `public`)
- `GET /api/mcp/artifacts/[id]` — returns `{ artifact, feedback[], signedUrl }` in one call; no visibility gate
- `POST /api/mcp/share` — delegates directly to `createShareLink` service
- `POST /api/mcp/summarize` — `artifact_id` + `force_refresh` in body; delegates to `getSummary`

**MCP server** (`mcp/` — ESM, Node.js stdio):
- `client.ts` — all calls add `x-api-key: ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY`; trailing slash stripped from base URL
- `list_artifacts` — formatted numbered list with type/visibility/tags/date
- `get_artifact` — full detail block with preview URL, feedback thread, status badges
- `create_share_link` — returns ready-to-paste URL + expiry date
- `summarize_feedback` — structured digest (overall assessment, open issues, suggestions, questions, approvals)

**To wire up Claude Desktop**, add to `claude_desktop_config.json`:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "artifact-hub": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["C:/path/to/repo/mcp/dist/index.js"],
      "env": {
        "ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY": "your-key",
        "ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL": "http://localhost:3000"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Ready for feedback — shall I proceed to Phase 5 (publish_artifact, add_feedback, update_feedback_status)?

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Artifact Hub Tracker

## Current phase

Phase 5 — MCP Extended

## Done

* Design plan completed (`docs/plans/2026-05-28-artifact-hub-design.md`)
* WRITEUP.md skeleton started with draft decision notes
* `requirements.md` present

### Phase 1 — Foundation (complete)

* Next.js 16 initialized (App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, ESLint)
* Dependencies installed: `@supabase/supabase-js`, `@supabase/ssr`, `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `nanoid`
* `supabase/migrations/001_initial.sql` — full schema: enums, `artifacts`, `feedback`, `share_links`, `feedback_summaries` tables with indexes
* `src/types/index.ts` — all domain types + service result helper
* `src/lib/supabase.ts` — browser client, SSR server client, admin client
* `src/lib/storage.ts` — signed URL generation (public 1h, share-link capped at `min(1h, remaining)`) + upload helper
* `src/lib/auth.ts` — `requireMcpAuth` / `isMcpAuthorized` for `/api/mcp/*` routes
* `.env.example` + `.env.local` template (no real values committed)
* `.gitignore` protects `node_modules`, `.next`, and all `.env*.local` files
* `npm run build` passes clean (TypeScript OK, no warnings)

### Phase 2 — Core Web (complete)

* `src/lib/services/artifacts.ts` — listArtifacts, getArtifact, createArtifact
* `src/lib/services/share.ts` — createShareLink, validateShareLink (expiry checked)
* API routes: `GET/POST /api/artifacts`, `GET /api/artifacts/[id]`, `GET /api/artifacts/[id]/preview`, `POST /api/share`, `GET /api/share/[token]`
* Components: ArtifactCard, GalleryFilter (URL-based filter state), ArtifactPreview (image/PDF/sandboxed HTML), ShareButton, PublishForm
* Pages: gallery (`/`), artifact detail (`/artifacts/[id]`, 403 for unlisted), share link view (`/share/[token]`), publish (`/publish`)
* Auto-share-link created on publish for unlisted artifacts; success screen shows copy-able URL
* `npm run build` clean — all 9 routes registered

### Phase 3 — Feedback + LLM (complete)

* `src/lib/services/feedback.ts` — listFeedback, addFeedback
* `src/lib/services/summarize.ts` — getCachedSummary, getSummary (cache-first: returns cached if feedback_count unchanged; calls Gemini + upserts when missing, stale, or force_refresh=true)
* API routes: `GET/POST /api/artifacts/[id]/feedback`, `POST /api/artifacts/[id]/summarize`
* Components: FeedbackList (server, type/status badges), FeedbackForm (client, router.refresh() on submit), FeedbackSummary (client, stale badge + generate/regenerate button, digest panels)
* Detail page and share link page both updated with feedback + summary sections
* Prompt version stored as "v1"; model name stored per summary row for transparency
* `npm run build` clean — 11 routes registered

### Pre-Phase-4 hardening (complete)

TypeScript reviewer findings addressed before MCP integration:
* **Gemini JSON validation** — `getCachedSummary` now propagates real DB errors (only returns null on PGRST116); `getSummary` distinguishes artifact 404 vs 500; `response.text` checked for empty before `JSON.parse`; parsed result validated with `isValidSummaryData` guard before storing
* **Enum input validation** — `POST /api/artifacts` validates `type` and `visibility` against allowed enum values, validates `tags` shape, builds typed `CreateArtifactBody` (no more `as any`); `POST /api/artifacts/[id]/feedback` validates `feedback_type` enum and builds typed `CreateFeedbackBody`
* **Pagination guard** — `GET /api/artifacts` returns 400 on non-integer or negative `limit`/`offset` (was passing NaN to Supabase)
* **`storage_path` stripped** — removed from all public API responses: `GET/POST /api/artifacts`, `GET /api/artifacts/[id]`, `GET /api/share/[token]`; `ArtifactPublic = Omit<Artifact, "storage_path">` added to types
* **`createShareLink` existence check** — service now verifies artifact exists before insert; bad `artifact_id` returns clean 404 instead of raw FK constraint error
* **Visibility on feedback GET** — `GET /api/artifacts/[id]/feedback` now checks visibility and returns 403 for unlisted artifacts (matching `/api/artifacts/[id]` policy); POST still allows feedback from share-link holders
* Build clean — all 11 routes, TypeScript OK

### Phase 4 — MCP Core Slice (complete)

API MCP routes (all require `x-api-key`, call same services as web routes, no visibility restriction):
* `GET /api/mcp/artifacts` — list with optional type/tags/visibility/limit/offset filters
* `GET /api/mcp/artifacts/[id]` — full detail: artifact + feedback list + 1-hour signed URL
* `POST /api/mcp/share` — create share link (delegates to createShareLink service)
* `POST /api/mcp/summarize` — cache-first summarize with artifact_id + force_refresh in body

MCP server (`mcp/` package — Node.js stdio, ESM):
* `mcp/src/client.ts` — HTTP helper reads `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY` + `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL`
* `mcp/src/tools/artifacts.ts` — `list_artifacts`, `get_artifact` (human-readable text output)
* `mcp/src/tools/share.ts` — `create_share_link`
* `mcp/src/tools/summarize.ts` — `summarize_feedback`
* `mcp/src/index.ts` — McpServer + StdioServerTransport entry point
* `mcp/tsconfig.json` — node16 module resolution, compiles to `mcp/dist/`
* `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL` added to `.env.example`
* `mcp/dist/` added to `.gitignore`
* Web `npm run build` clean — 15 routes (4 new MCP routes registered)
* `cd mcp && npm run build` clean — 6 JS files compiled, TypeScript OK

## In progress

* Phase 5 — MCP Extended (not started yet)

## Blockers and decisions

* **LLM provider switched to Gemini** — `@anthropic-ai/sdk` removed; `@google/genai` installed. Summarization will use `GEMINI_API_KEY` + `GEMINI_MODEL` (default `gemini-2.5-flash`). Decision: avoid unnecessary Anthropic API costs; Gemini Flash is sufficient for text-only feedback summarization.
* **Manual Supabase setup required before Phase 2 can use real data:**
  1. Create a Supabase project
  2. Run `supabase/migrations/001_initial.sql` (`npx supabase db push`)
  3. Create a private `artifacts` storage bucket
  4. Fill in `.env.local` with real credentials (`GEMINI_API_KEY` instead of `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`)
* No code blockers

  2. Run `supabase/migrations/001_initial.sql` (`npx supabase db push`)
  3. Create a private `artifacts` storage bucket
  4. Fill in `.env.local` with real credentials (`GEMINI_API_KEY` instead of `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`)
* No code blockers

lines: * Web `npm run build` clean — 15 routes (4 new MCP routes registered)
* `cd mcp && npm run build` clean — 6 JS files compiled, TypeScript OK
+* `POST /api/mcp/artifacts` — publish artifact; same validation + auto share-link logic as web route
+* `POST /api/mcp/feedback` — add feedback (no visibility gate; MCP key = access grant)
+* `mcp/src/tools/publish.ts` — `publish_artifact` (returns URL or share link based on visibility)
+* Web build clean — 17 routes; MCP `tsc` clean
+* Phase 6 — Seed + Deploy + Verification (not started yet)
+* `mcp/README.md` — Claude Desktop config snippet, tool list, example conversation (Phase 6)

* **LLM provider switched to Gemini** — `@anthropic-ai/sdk` removed; `@google/genai` installed. Summarization will use `GEMINI_API_KEY` + `GEMINI_MODEL` (default `gemini-2.5-flash`). Decision: avoid unnecessary Anthropic API costs; Gemini Flash is sufficient for text-only feedback summarization.

lastPrompt: Proceed with Phase 5. make sure the Claude Desktop config example is saved somewhere later, either in WRITEUP.md or mcp/README.md, not just in chat. But that can wait until final docs.

Phase 5 complete. All 7 MCP tools are now implemented:

| Tool | Route | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `list_artifacts` | `GET /api/mcp/artifacts` | Full filter support including visibility |
| `get_artifact` | `GET /api/mcp/artifacts/[id]` | Feedback + signed URL, visibility bypassed |
| `create_share_link` | `POST /api/mcp/share` | Any artifact |
| `summarize_feedback` | `POST /api/mcp/summarize` | Cache-first, force_refresh supported |
| `publish_artifact` | `POST /api/mcp/artifacts` | Base64 upload, auto share-link for unlisted |
| `add_feedback` | `POST /api/mcp/feedback` | No visibility gate |
| `update_feedback_status` | `PATCH /api/mcp/feedback` | MCP/API-only, not in web UI |

The Claude Desktop config snippet is noted in `docs/TRACKER.md` as pending for Phase 6 docs. Ready to proceed to Phase 6 (seed data, deploy, WRITEUP) whenever you are.

message.content:
Before starting Phase 6, run a focused MCP workflow review of Phases 4 and 5.

Review only the MCP implementation and its API adapter routes. Do not make code changes yet.

Check:

All 7 tools are registered correctly
Tool names and descriptions feel conversational, not just CRUD
Tool parameters are clear and reviewer-friendly
Tool responses are useful inside Claude Desktop
/mcp does not duplicate business logic
/api/mcp/* routes are thin and reuse service functions
x-api-key is required consistently
MCP can access unlisted artifacts only through the API-key path
Error messages are clear enough for a reviewer using Claude Desktop
The Claude Desktop config example is accurate

Return:

Blockers to fix before Phase 6
Important improvements worth fixing now
Polish that can wait

Do not suggest broad product changes, CI, tests, rate limiting, or new features.

input.description: MCP implementation review — Phases 4 & 5
input.prompt:
Read-only review of the MCP implementation across Phases 4 and 5 of a Next.js project called Artifact Hub. Do NOT suggest code changes — return a prioritized findings list only.

Repo root: C:\Users\zahid\Documents\Github\ezra-coaching

## What to review

**MCP server** (stdio, Node.js ESM):
- `mcp/src/index.ts` — entry point, tool registration
- `mcp/src/client.ts` — HTTP helper (adds x-api-key header)
- `mcp/src/tools/artifacts.ts` — list_artifacts, get_artifact
- `mcp/src/tools/share.ts` — create_share_link
- `mcp/src/tools/summarize.ts` — summarize_feedback
- `mcp/src/tools/publish.ts` — publish_artifact
- `mcp/src/tools/feedback.ts` — add_feedback, update_feedback_status

**API adapter routes** (Next.js App Router, all under /api/mcp/*):
- `src/app/api/mcp/artifacts/route.ts` — GET list + POST publish
- `src/app/api/mcp/artifacts/[id]/route.ts` — GET detail (artifact + feedback + signed URL)
- `src/app/api/mcp/share/route.ts` — POST create share link
- `src/app/api/mcp/summarize/route.ts` — POST summarize
- `src/app/api/mcp/feedback/route.ts` — POST add + PATCH update status

**Auth helper** (already implemented):
- `src/lib/auth.ts` — requireMcpAuth / isMcpAuthorized

**Service layer** (already reviewed separately — treat as correct):
- `src/lib/services/artifacts.ts`
- `src/lib/services/feedback.ts`
- `src/lib/services/share.ts`
- `src/lib/services/summarize.ts`
- `src/lib/storage.ts`

## What to check

1. **All 7 tools registered** — list_artifacts, get_artifact, create_share_link, summarize_feedback, publish_artifact, add_feedback, update_feedback_status
2. **Tool UX** — names/descriptions feel conversational for a reviewer in Claude Desktop (not just CRUD labels); parameter descriptions guide the user; response text is readable in a chat window
3. **No business logic in MCP tools** — tools should only format inputs, call the HTTP adapter, and format output; any logic belongs in services
4. **Thin API routes** — /api/mcp/* handlers should: check auth, parse/validate input, call a service function, return JSON — nothing more
5. **Auth consistency** — every /api/mcp/* handler calls requireMcpAuth before any other work
6. **Visibility bypass** — MCP routes must NOT enforce the public/unlisted visibility check (web routes do; MCP routes bypass it — key = access grant)
7. **storage_path** — must not appear in any /api/mcp/* JSON response
8. **Error messages** — errors returned from /api/mcp/* routes should be clear enough for Claude to relay to a reviewer without being confusing
9. **Claude Desktop config accuracy** — the MCP server reads ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY and ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL; verify these match what client.ts actually reads
10. **No logic duplication** — check whether any business logic from services is re-implemented inside the route handlers or MCP tools

## What NOT to check
- The web routes (/api/artifacts/*, /api/share/*, etc.) — those were reviewed separately
- The service layer internals — treat as correct
- Tests, CI, rate limiting, deployment, new features
- The supabase client or storage helper internals

## Output format

Return exactly three sections:

**1. Blockers** — must fix before Phase 6 (seed + deploy + WRITEUP)
**2. Important** — worth fixing before Phase 6
**3. Polish** — can wait

For each finding: file:line reference, what the problem is, concrete fix. Keep it tight.

content.text:
I now have everything I need for a complete review. TypeScript compiles clean in both packages. ESLint on the Next.js project shows one unrelated error (`Date.now` in a Server Component that is outside the review scope) and only `_` unused-variable warnings that are the intentional destructure idiom for stripping `storage_path`. All 7 tools are registered. Here are the findings:

---

## 1. Blockers

**`WRITEUP.md:49` — Wrong env var name in draft copy**

The WRITEUP.md draft notes instruct the reviewer to configure Claude Desktop with `ARTIFACT_HUB_API_KEY`. The actual env var consumed by `mcp/src/client.ts:3` and `src/lib/auth.ts:7` is `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY`. If a reviewer follows the draft note as written, their MCP config will silently use an empty string for the API key, every request will get 401, and they will have no idea why. The same stale name appears in `docs/plans/2026-05-28-artifact-hub-design.md:69`. Both places must be corrected to `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY` before the WRITEUP is finalised and the demo key is published.

**`mcp/src/client.ts:3` — Missing API key produces silent 401 loop, not a startup error**

`process.env.ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY ?? ""` defaults to an empty string. If the env var is not set in Claude Desktop config, every tool call returns 401, but the MCP server starts without complaint and gives the user no actionable message. The server should log a warning (to stderr, not stdout — stdio transport uses stdout) and/or throw at startup when the key is absent, so the reviewer immediately knows the config is wrong rather than debugging opaque 401 errors from tool responses.

---

## 2. Important

**`mcp/src/tools/share.ts:34` and `mcp/src/tools/publish.ts:45` — `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL` read a second time instead of using the value already resolved in `client.ts`**

`client.ts` already reads and normalises `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL` into a module-level `baseUrl` constant, but it is not exported. The share and publish tools each re-read and re-normalise the same env var inline, duplicating the default and the trailing-slash logic. If the env var name ever changes, there are three call sites to update. Export `baseUrl` from `client.ts` and import it in those two tools.

**`src/app/api/mcp/artifacts/[id]/route.ts:35` — Silent feedback failure is invisible to the tool**

When `listFeedback` fails, `feedbackResult.ok` is false and the route silently substitutes `[]` with no error signal. The MCP tool receives `feedback: []` and renders "No feedback yet." — indistinguishable from an artifact that genuinely has no feedback. Consider returning a non-2xx status or including an `"feedbackError"` field so the tool can communicate the degradation to the reviewer rather than giving a false empty state.

**`src/app/api/mcp/feedback/route.ts:41-43` — Extra round-trip to confirm artifact existence on every `add_feedback`**

The route fetches the full artifact via `getArtifact` before calling `addFeedback`, purely to verify existence. The `addFeedback` service will itself fail with a foreign-key violation (404 or 500 from Supabase) if the artifact does not exist. The extra query is a preventive guard, but it doubles the database cost for every feedback submission. This is not incorrect, but the cost should be a conscious choice; if `addFeedback` already surfaces a clean 404, the guard can be removed.

**`src/lib/auth.ts:9` — String comparison is not timing-safe**

`key === expected` is a direct string equality check. For a long-lived admin API key used in a demo environment accessible from the internet, timing attacks are low-risk but still real. `crypto.timingSafeEqual` (available without any import in Node.js/Next.js Edge/Node runtime) would be the correct tool here. Worth fixing before the public deploy rather than after.

---

## 3. Polish

**`mcp/src/tools/artifacts.ts:57` — `URLSearchParams.size` requires Node 19+**

`params.size` was added in Node.js 19. The `mcp/package.json` specifies no `engines` field, so a reviewer on Node 18 LTS would get `undefined > 0 === false` and always omit the query string, silently making every `list_artifacts` call ignore all filters. Either add `"engines": { "node": ">=19" }` to `mcp/package.json` or replace with `params.toString().length > 0` which works everywhere.

**`mcp/src/tools/feedback.ts:50` — `update_feedback_status` description uses a code-style transition label**

The description reads "open → resolved → needs_review" which implies these are sequential states. They are not — any status can transition to any other. The arrow notation will mislead reviewers in Claude Desktop. Reword to something like "Set the status to open, resolved, or needs_review. Any transition is permitted."

**`mcp/src/tools/summarize.ts:71` — Empty string lines in the filter could swallow `approval_count: 0` correctly but drop the "Approvals:" line for count = 0**

`approvalLine` is only appended when `s.approval_count > 0`, so zero approvals are intentionally omitted. That is a reasonable UX choice, but it means a reviewer cannot distinguish "zero approvals" from "field missing from Gemini output." If completeness matters for the WRITEUP demo, add `Approvals: 0` unconditionally.

**`mcp/src/tools/artifacts.ts:88` — `get_artifact` interface declares `signedUrl: string` as always-present**

The route always includes the `signedUrl` key in the JSON response (even when empty — it sends `""`), so the cast is technically safe. But the empty-string case is handled by the truthiness check `if (signedUrl)` at line 107, meaning the interface and the guard are slightly misaligned. Typing it as `signedUrl: string | null` in the interface and returning `null` (not `""`) from the route when URL generation fails would make the contract explicit.

**No `mcp/README.md` exists**

There is no setup guide for the MCP package. The WRITEUP will need to include the Claude Desktop config snippet (command, args, env). Having it in `mcp/README.md` as well means a reviewer who clones the repo can self-serve without waiting for the WRITEUP. Low priority since the WRITEUP will cover it, but worth adding before Phase 6 is complete.

content.text:
Good review. Here's the prioritized output:

---

## Blockers — Fix before Phase 6

**B1 — Wrong env var name in `WRITEUP.md` and design doc**
`WRITEUP.md:49` and `docs/plans/2026-05-28-artifact-hub-design.md:69` both say `ARTIFACT_HUB_API_KEY`. The actual var consumed by `mcp/src/client.ts` and `src/lib/auth.ts` is `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY`. A reviewer following the draft notes would get silent 401s on every tool call with no actionable error.

**B2 — Missing API key starts silently, produces opaque 401 errors**
`mcp/src/client.ts:3` — `process.env.ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY ?? ""` defaults to empty string. If the reviewer's Claude Desktop config omits the env var, every tool call returns 401 with no startup warning. The server should log a warning to stderr (not stdout — stdio transport owns stdout) when the key is absent.

---

## Important — Worth fixing before Phase 6

**I1 — `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL` read in three places instead of one**
`mcp/src/client.ts` normalises the URL into a module-level constant but doesn't export it. `mcp/src/tools/share.ts:34` and `mcp/src/tools/publish.ts:45` each re-read and re-normalise the same env var inline. Export `baseUrl` from `client.ts` and import it.

**I2 — Silent feedback failure renders as "No feedback yet."**
`src/app/api/mcp/artifacts/[id]/route.ts:35` — when `listFeedback` fails, the route substitutes `[]` silently. The tool outputs "No feedback yet." — indistinguishable from a genuine empty artifact. Surface a degradation signal (`feedbackError` field, or log it) so the tool can communicate partial failure.

**I3 — `URLSearchParams.size` requires Node 19+**
`mcp/src/tools/artifacts.ts:57` — `.size` was added in Node 19. On Node 18 LTS it returns `undefined`, so the condition is always false and all filters are silently dropped. Replace with `params.toString().length > 0` or add `"engines": { "node": ">=19" }` to `mcp/package.json`.

**I4 — Timing-unsafe API key comparison**
`src/lib/auth.ts:9` — `key === expected` before a public deploy. Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual`.

---

## Polish — Can wait

| # | File | Issue |
|---|---|---|
| P1 | `mcp/src/tools/feedback.ts:50` | "open → resolved → needs_review" implies sequential states; transitions are free-form — reword |
| P2 | `mcp/src/tools/summarize.ts:71` | `approval_count: 0` silently omitted from output — reviewer can't distinguish zero approvals from missing field |
| P3 | `mcp/src/tools/artifacts.ts:88` | `signedUrl` typed as `string` but route sends `""` on failure — better to type as `string \| null` and return `null` from the route |
| P4 | `mcp/` | No `README.md` — noted as pending in TRACKER.md; needed before Phase 6 complete |
| P5 | `src/app/api/mcp/feedback/route.ts` | Extra `getArtifact` round-trip on every `add_feedback` — service FK error would surface a clean enough error on its own |

---

Shall I fix B1–B2 and I1–I4 now before Phase 6?

message.content:
Yes, fix B1, B2, and I1–I4 before Phase 6.

Make only these targeted changes:

Replace all incorrect ARTIFACT_HUB_API_KEY references with ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY
Add an MCP startup warning to stderr if ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY is missing. Do not write warnings to stdout because stdio transport owns stdout
Export the normalized baseUrl from mcp/src/client.ts and reuse it instead of re-reading ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL in multiple tools
Do not silently convert feedback fetch failures into an empty feedback list. Surface a clear partial-failure signal in the MCP response
Replace URLSearchParams.size with a Node 18-compatible check such as params.toString().length > 0
Use crypto.timingSafeEqual for API key comparison in src/lib/auth.ts

If it is a one-line/simple fix, also ensure approval_count: 0 is displayed instead of omitted.

Do not work on the other polish items now. Do not start Phase 6 until these fixes are complete, validation passes, docs/TRACKER.md is updated, and the fix is committed.

# Artifact Hub — Writeup

> **Status: DRAFT — decision log only. Final writeup to be completed after implementation.**

---

## What I built and why

_[Final writeup: describe the running system, key flows, and product feel. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft decisions:**
- Chose a frictionless demo workspace — reviewers can browse, leave feedback, summarize, share, and publish test artifacts at the deployed URL without any login or key setup. The reviewer experience was the primary UX constraint.
- Prioritized a polished end-to-end artifact lifecycle (publish → browse → detail → feedback → summarize → share) over broad feature count. A complete, working core is worth more than a sprawling system with gaps.
- Skipped NL search to keep the LLM story focused on one feature that directly addresses the stated pain point.

---

## What I chose not to build and why

_[Final writeup: enumerate cuts and rationale. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft decisions:**
- **No user auth or RBAC** — full auth adds 4–6 hours of complexity for a 2-day build. Access control is still demonstrated through the public/unlisted visibility model, private Supabase Storage with signed URLs, expiring share link tokens, and API-key-protected MCP routes. A reviewer can observe the access model working without needing to sign in.
- **No edit or delete of artifacts** — append-only publish keeps the data model and UI simple. Not needed for the evaluation scope.
- **No NL search** — removed in favour of tag/type filtering, which is sufficient for a demo-sized catalog. Keeping one LLM feature sharp is better than two shallow ones.
- **No rate limiting** — demo environment only; noted here so it is not mistaken for an oversight.
- **`update_feedback_status` is MCP/API-only** — changing a feedback item's status (open → resolved → needs_review) is a trusted review-management action. Anonymous public users should not be able to close or re-open issues left by others. Restricting this to API-key-protected MCP routes keeps the trust boundary explicit without needing full auth.

---

## Architecture overview

_[Final writeup: system diagram or prose description. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft notes:**
- Next.js App Router on Vercel — single deployable unit
- Supabase Postgres for four tables: `artifacts`, `feedback`, `share_links`, `feedback_summaries`
- Supabase Storage — private bucket only; all signed URLs generated server-side after access checks
- Visibility model: `public` artifacts appear in the gallery and at `/artifacts/[id]`; `unlisted` artifacts return 403 at that route and are only accessible via `/share/[token]`
- Separated normal web API routes (`/api/*`, open for demo) from protected MCP adapter routes (`/api/mcp/*`, require `x-api-key`). Both call the same `src/lib/services/` functions — no logic duplication.

---

## How the MCP integration works

_[Final writeup: MCP config snippet, tool list, example conversation. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft notes:**
- MCP server is a Node.js stdio process in `/mcp`. Reviewer adds it to Claude Desktop config with `ARTIFACT_HUB_API_KEY` and `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL` env vars.
- MCP tools call `/api/mcp/*` HTTP adapter routes. The API key is the access grant — MCP callers can list and access unlisted artifacts, bypass the visibility check, and perform write actions.
- MCP is on the critical path. Minimum required tools verified before final deploy: `list_artifacts`, `get_artifact`, `create_share_link`, `summarize_feedback`. Then: `publish_artifact`, `add_feedback`, `update_feedback_status`.
- 7 tools total: `list_artifacts`, `get_artifact`, `publish_artifact`, `add_feedback`, `update_feedback_status`, `create_share_link`, `summarize_feedback`.

---

## Where and why I used LLM capabilities

_[Final writeup: describe the summarization feature, prompt design, caching behaviour. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft notes:**
- **Feedback summarization** — chosen because it directly addresses the stated pain point: feedback scattered across Slack threads with no synthesis. On any artifact detail page, "Summarize Feedback" produces a structured digest: overall assessment, open issues, suggestions, questions, approval count.
- Powered by **Gemini** (`@google/genai`, model: `gemini-2.5-flash` by default). Text-only input — no grounding, images, or audio needed.
- Cache-first: result stored in `feedback_summaries` with `feedback_count` at generation time. If current count matches, the cached summary is returned without calling Gemini. Only regenerates when feedback has been added since the last summary, or `force_refresh` is set.
- `model` and `prompt_version` are stored with each summary for transparency in this writeup.
- Feature is immediately testable from seeded artifacts (pre-populated with feedback across all four types).

---

## Deployment approach

_[Final writeup: live URL, env var list, Supabase project details. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft notes:**
- Next.js app deployed to Vercel from this repo
- Supabase hosted project (Postgres + private Storage bucket)
- Required env vars: `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_MODEL`

---

## Demo admin key

_[Fill in after deployment — document the demo API key here for the reviewer's MCP config and direct API testing.]_

---

## What I'd do next with another week

_[Final writeup: honest prioritized list. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft ideas:**
- User accounts with artifact ownership (Supabase Auth)
- NL search using pgvector embeddings
- Feedback status management in the web UI (currently MCP/API-only)
- Artifact versioning
- Rate limiting on feedback and publish endpoints

Fixes from MCP workflow review before Phase 6:

* **I2 — feedback failure surface** — `GET /api/mcp/artifacts/[id]` now returns `feedbackError: string | null`; `get_artifact` tool renders "(Could not load feedback: …)" and "Feedback (unavailable):" header instead of silent empty list

* **P2 — approval_count: 0** — always shown in `summarize_feedback` output (was omitted when zero)

* `requirements.md` present

### Phase 1 — Foundation (complete)

* Next.js 16 initialized (App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, ESLint)
* Dependencies installed: `@supabase/supabase-js`, `@supabase/ssr`, `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `nanoid`
* `supabase/migrations/001_initial.sql` — full schema: enums, `artifacts`, `feedback`, `share_links`, `feedback_summaries` tables with indexes
* `src/types/index.ts` — all domain types + service result helper
* `src/lib/supabase.ts` — browser client, SSR server client, admin client
* `src/lib/storage.ts` — signed URL generation (public 1h, share-link capped at `min(1h, remaining)`) + upload helper
* `src/lib/auth.ts` — `requireMcpAuth` / `isMcpAuthorized` for `/api/mcp/*` routes
* `.env.example` + `.env.local` template (no real values committed)
* `.gitignore` protects `node_modules`, `.next`, and all `.env*.local` files
* `npm run build` passes clean (TypeScript OK, no warnings)

### Phase 2 — Core Web (complete)

* `src/lib/services/artifacts.ts` — listArtifacts, getArtifact, createArtifact
* `src/lib/services/share.ts` — createShareLink, validateShareLink (expiry checked)
* API routes: `GET/POST /api/artifacts`, `GET /api/artifacts/[id]`, `GET /api/artifacts/[id]/preview`, `POST /api/share`, `GET /api/share/[token]`
* Components: ArtifactCard, GalleryFilter (URL-based filter state), ArtifactPreview (image/PDF/sandboxed HTML), ShareButton, PublishForm
* Pages: gallery (`/`), artifact detail (`/artifacts/[id]`, 403 for unlisted), share link view (`/share/[token]`), publish (`/publish`)
* Auto-share-link created on publish for unlisted artifacts; success screen shows copy-able URL
* `npm run build` clean — all 9 routes registered

### Phase 3 — Feedback + LLM (complete)

* `src/lib/services/feedback.ts` — listFeedback, addFeedback
* `src/lib/services/summarize.ts` — getCachedSummary, getSummary (cache-first: returns cached if feedback_count unchanged; calls Gemini + upserts when missing, stale, or force_refresh=true)
* API routes: `GET/POST /api/artifacts/[id]/feedback`, `POST /api/artifacts/[id]/summarize`
* Components: FeedbackList (server, type/status badges), FeedbackForm (client, router.refresh() on submit), FeedbackSummary (client, stale badge + generate/regenerate button, digest panels)
* Detail page and share link page both updated with feedback + summary sections
* Prompt version stored as "v1"; model name stored per summary row for transparency
* `npm run build` clean — 11 routes registered

### Pre-Phase-4 hardening (complete)

TypeScript reviewer findings addressed before MCP integration:
* **Gemini JSON validation** — `getCachedSummary` now propagates real DB errors (only returns null on PGRST116); `getSummary` distinguishes artifact 404 vs 500; `response.text` checked for empty before `JSON.parse`; parsed result validated with `isValidSummaryData` guard before storing
* **Enum input validation** — `POST /api/artifacts` validates `type` and `visibility` against allowed enum values, validates `tags` shape, builds typed `CreateArtifactBody` (no more `as any`); `POST /api/artifacts/[id]/feedback` validates `feedback_type` enum and builds typed `CreateFeedbackBody`
* **Pagination guard** — `GET /api/artifacts` returns 400 on non-integer or negative `limit`/`offset` (was passing NaN to Supabase)
* **`storage_path` stripped** — removed from all public API responses: `GET/POST /api/artifacts`, `GET /api/artifacts/[id]`, `GET /api/share/[token]`; `ArtifactPublic = Omit<Artifact, "storage_path">` added to types
* **`createShareLink` existence check** — service now verifies artifact exists before insert; bad `artifact_id` returns clean 404 instead of raw FK constraint error
* **Visibility on feedback GET** — `GET /api/artifacts/[id]/feedback` now checks visibility and returns 403 for unlisted artifacts (matching `/api/artifacts/[id]` policy); POST still allows feedback from share-link holders
* Build clean — all 11 routes, TypeScript OK

### Phase 4 — MCP Core Slice (complete)

API MCP routes (all require `x-api-key`, call same services as web routes, no visibility restriction):
* `GET /api/mcp/artifacts` — list with optional type/tags/visibility/limit/offset filters
* `GET /api/mcp/artifacts/[id]` — full detail: artifact + feedback list + 1-hour signed URL
* `POST /api/mcp/share` — create share link (delegates to createShareLink service)
* `POST /api/mcp/summarize` — cache-first summarize with artifact_id + force_refresh in body

MCP server (`mcp/` package — Node.js stdio, ESM):
* `mcp/src/client.ts` — HTTP helper reads `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY` + `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL`
* `mcp/src/tools/artifacts.ts` — `list_artifacts`, `get_artifact` (human-readable text output)
* `mcp/src/tools/share.ts` — `create_share_link`
* `mcp/src/tools/summarize.ts` — `summarize_feedback`
* `mcp/src/index.ts` — McpServer + StdioServerTransport entry point
* `mcp/tsconfig.json` — node16 module resolution, compiles to `mcp/dist/`
* `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL` added to `.env.example`
* `mcp/dist/` added to `.gitignore`
* Web `npm run build` clean — 15 routes (4 new MCP routes registered)
* `cd mcp && npm run build` clean — 6 JS files compiled, TypeScript OK

### Phase 5 — MCP Extended (complete)

* `src/lib/services/feedback.ts` — added `updateFeedbackStatus` (PGRST116 → 404, else 500)
* `POST /api/mcp/artifacts` — publish artifact; same validation + auto share-link logic as web route
* `POST /api/mcp/feedback` — add feedback (no visibility gate; MCP key = access grant)
* `PATCH /api/mcp/feedback` — update feedback status (MCP/API-only)
* `mcp/src/client.ts` — added `patch()` helper
* `mcp/src/tools/publish.ts` — `publish_artifact` (returns URL or share link based on visibility)
* `mcp/src/tools/feedback.ts` — `add_feedback`, `update_feedback_status`
* All 7 MCP tools registered in `mcp/src/index.ts`
* Web build clean — 17 routes; MCP `tsc` clean

## In progress

* Phase 6 — Seed + Deploy + Verification (not started yet)

## Pending

* `mcp/README.md` — Claude Desktop config snippet, tool list, example conversation (Phase 6)

## Blockers and decisions

* **LLM provider switched to Gemini** — `@anthropic-ai/sdk` removed; `@google/genai` installed. Summarization will use `GEMINI_API_KEY` + `GEMINI_MODEL` (default `gemini-2.5-flash`). Decision: avoid unnecessary Anthropic API costs; Gemini Flash is sufficient for text-only feedback summarization.
* **Manual Supabase setup required before Phase 2 can use real data:**
  1. Create a Supabase project
  2. Run `supabase/migrations/001_initial.sql` (`npx supabase db push`)
  3. Create a private `artifacts` storage bucket
  4. Fill in `.env.local` with real credentials (`GEMINI_API_KEY` instead of `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`)
* No code blockers

lines: * Web build clean — 17 routes; MCP `tsc` clean
+Fixes from MCP workflow review before Phase 6:
+* **B1 — env var name** — `ARTIFACT_HUB_API_KEY` → `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY` in `WRITEUP.md` and design doc; now matches `mcp/src/client.ts` and `src/lib/auth.ts`

+* **B2 — startup warning** — `mcp/src/index.ts` writes to stderr (not stdout) when `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY` or `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL` is missing, so misconfigured Claude Desktop shows a clear error instead of opaque 401s
+* **I1 — baseUrl deduplication** — exported from `mcp/src/client.ts`; `share.ts` and `publish.ts` import it instead of re-reading the env var inline

+* **I2 — feedback failure surface** — `GET /api/mcp/artifacts/[id]` now returns `feedbackError: string | null`; `get_artifact` tool renders "(Could not load feedback: …)" and "Feedback (unavailable):" header instead of silent empty list
+* **I3 — URLSearchParams.size** — replaced with `params.toString().length > 0` for Node 18 LTS compatibility
+* **I4 — timing-safe auth** — `src/lib/auth.ts` uses `crypto.timingSafeEqual` with same-length guard
+* **P2 — approval_count: 0** — always shown in `summarize_feedback` output (was omitted when zero)
+* Both builds clean: web (17 routes), mcp (tsc)
* Phase 6 — Seed + Deploy + Verification (not started yet)

Base directory for this skill: C:\Users\zahid\.claude\plugins\cache\superpowers-marketplace\superpowers\4.0.2\skills\executing-plans

# Executing Plans

## Overview

Load plan, review critically, execute tasks in batches, report for review between batches.

**Core principle:** Batch execution with checkpoints for architect review.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the executing-plans skill to implement this plan."

## The Process

### Step 1: Load and Review Plan
1. Read plan file
2. Review critically - identify any questions or concerns about the plan
3. If concerns: Raise them with your human partner before starting
4. If no concerns: Create TodoWrite and proceed

### Step 2: Execute Batch
**Default: First 3 tasks**

For each task:
1. Mark as in_progress
2. Follow each step exactly (plan has bite-sized steps)
3. Run verifications as specified
4. Mark as completed

### Step 3: Report
When batch complete:
- Show what was implemented
- Show verification output
- Say: "Ready for feedback."

### Step 4: Continue
Based on feedback:
- Apply changes if needed
- Execute next batch
- Repeat until complete

### Step 5: Complete Development

After all tasks complete and verified:
- Announce: "I'm using the finishing-a-development-branch skill to complete this work."
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch
- Follow that skill to verify tests, present options, execute choice

## When to Stop and Ask for Help

**STOP executing immediately when:**
- Hit a blocker mid-batch (missing dependency, test fails, instruction unclear)
- Plan has critical gaps preventing starting
- You don't understand an instruction
- Verification fails repeatedly

**Ask for clarification rather than guessing.**

## When to Revisit Earlier Steps

**Return to Review (Step 1) when:**
- Partner updates the plan based on your feedback
- Fundamental approach needs rethinking

**Don't force through blockers** - stop and ask.

## Remember
- Review plan critically first
- Follow plan steps exactly
- Don't skip verifications
- Reference skills when plan says to
- Between batches: just report and wait
- Stop when blocked, don't guess

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Project
6	
7	Artifact Hub — a platform for publishing, browsing, reviewing, and sharing AI-generated content. Full design: `docs/plans/2026-05-28-artifact-hub-design.md`.
8	
9	## Execution tracking
10	
11	`docs/TRACKER.md` is the source of truth for execution state.
12	
13	- At the start of each phase: read `docs/TRACKER.md`, confirm the current phase and active tasks, and list files you plan to create or modify before touching anything
14	- During implementation: update `docs/TRACKER.md` only after meaningful milestones, blockers, decisions, or phase completion — not as a running diary
15	- At the end of each phase: run relevant validation checks, summarize what changed, update `docs/TRACKER.md`, and commit the completed phase
16	
17	## Commands
18	
19	```bash
20	# Web app (Next.js — run from repo root)
21	npm run dev          # dev server on localhost:3000
22	npm run build        # production build
23	npm run lint         # ESLint
24	
25	# MCP server
26	cd mcp && npm run build   # compile TypeScript
27	cd mcp && npm run start   # run stdio server (for testing outside Claude Desktop)
28	
29	# Database
30	npx supabase db push
31	# DESTRUCTIVE — only run after explicit confirmation:
32	npx supabase db reset --linked   # wipes hosted project and re-seeds
33	```
34	
35	## Architecture
36	
37	Two packages: `src/` (Next.js app, deploys to Vercel) and `mcp/` (Node.js stdio MCP server, run locally by reviewer).
38	
39	**Request path for all features:**
40	- Web routes (`/api/*`) — open for demo use, no auth
41	- MCP adapter routes (`/api/mcp/*`) — require `x-api-key` header, checked in `src/lib/auth.ts`
42	- Both route groups call the same `src/lib/services/` functions — no logic lives in route handlers
43	
44	**Storage:** All artifacts in a single private Supabase bucket. Signed URLs are generated server-side in `src/lib/storage.ts` and only after the relevant access check passes. Never expose raw bucket paths or public URLs.
45	
46	**Visibility enforcement:**
47	- `public` artifacts: gallery + `/artifacts/[id]`
48	- `unlisted` artifacts: `/artifacts/[id]` returns 403; only accessible via `/share/[token]` or MCP routes (key = access grant)
49	- When a new unlisted artifact is published from the web UI, a 30-day share link is auto-created and shown on the success screen — otherwise the publisher would have no way to view it
50	
51	**Feedback summarization (the LLM feature):**
52	- Entry point: `src/lib/services/summarize.ts`
53	- Cache-first: compare `feedback_summaries.feedback_count` against current `COUNT(feedback)`. Return cached if equal. Call Gemini only when missing, stale, or `force_refresh: true`.
54	- Result stored in `feedback_summaries` as JSONB with `model`, `prompt_version`, `feedback_count`, `generated_at`
55	- Summary shape: `{ overall_assessment, open_issues[], suggestions[], questions[], approval_count }`
56	
57	**MCP server** (`/mcp/src/index.ts`): stdio transport, 7 tools. Tools call `/api/mcp/*` with the API key from env. Minimum working set: `list_artifacts`, `get_artifact`, `create_share_link`, `summarize_feedback`. `update_feedback_status` is MCP/API-only — not exposed in the web UI.
58	
59	## Secret safety
60	
61	- Never print, commit, or paste real environment variable values
62	- Use `.env.example` for variable names only — no values
63	- Do not expose `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY`, or any Vercel tokens in logs, screenshots, commits, or `WRITEUP.md`
64	
65	## Key env vars
66	
67	| Var | Used by |
68	|---|---|
69	| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` | Browser + server |
70	| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | Browser |
71	| `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` | Server only (storage + admin queries) |
72	| `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY` | `/api/mcp/*` auth check + MCP server config |
73	| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | `summarize.ts` service |
74	| `GEMINI_MODEL` | `summarize.ts` service (defaults to `gemini-2.5-flash` if unset) |
75	
76	## Database
77	
78	Four tables: `artifacts`, `feedback`, `share_links`, `feedback_summaries`. Migration in `supabase/migrations/001_initial.sql`. Seed data in `supabase/seed.sql` + `supabase/seed-files/` (3 public artifacts, 4–6 feedback entries each — enough for summarization to produce a meaningful digest on first load).
79	
80	## Constraints to carry forward
81	
82	- Never generate a signed URL before the access check passes
83	- Route handlers are thin; all logic goes in `src/lib/services/`
84	- No logic duplication between `/api/*` and `/api/mcp/*` routes
85	- Summarization is always cache-first — do not call Gemini if `feedback_count` is unchanged
86	- Append-only artifacts: no edit or delete endpoints
87	- Work one phase at a time — do not start the next phase until the current phase is validated, `docs/TRACKER.md` is updated, and changes are committed
88	- Before broad architectural changes, explain the tradeoff and ask for confirmation

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project

Artifact Hub — a platform for publishing, browsing, reviewing, and sharing AI-generated content. Full design: `docs/plans/2026-05-28-artifact-hub-design.md`.

## Execution tracking

`docs/TRACKER.md` is the source of truth for execution state.

- At the start of each phase: read `docs/TRACKER.md`, confirm the current phase and active tasks, and list files you plan to create or modify before touching anything
- During implementation: update `docs/TRACKER.md` only after meaningful milestones, blockers, decisions, or phase completion — not as a running diary
- At the end of each phase: run relevant validation checks, summarize what changed, update `docs/TRACKER.md`, and commit the completed phase

## Commands

```bash
# Web app (Next.js — run from repo root)
npm run dev          # dev server on localhost:3000
npm run build        # production build
npm run lint         # ESLint

# MCP server
cd mcp && npm run build   # compile TypeScript
cd mcp && npm run start   # run stdio server (for testing outside Claude Desktop)

# Database
npx supabase db push
# DESTRUCTIVE — only run after explicit confirmation:
npx supabase db reset --linked   # wipes hosted project and re-seeds
```

## Architecture

Two packages: `src/` (Next.js app, deploys to Vercel) and `mcp/` (Node.js stdio MCP server, run locally by reviewer).

**Request path for all features:**
- Web routes (`/api/*`) — open for demo use, no auth
- MCP adapter routes (`/api/mcp/*`) — require `x-api-key` header, checked in `src/lib/auth.ts`
- Both route groups call the same `src/lib/services/` functions — no logic lives in route handlers

**Storage:** All artifacts in a single private Supabase bucket. Signed URLs are generated server-side in `src/lib/storage.ts` and only after the relevant access check passes. Never expose raw bucket paths or public URLs.

**Visibility enforcement:**
- `public` artifacts: gallery + `/artifacts/[id]`
- `unlisted` artifacts: `/artifacts/[id]` returns 403; only accessible via `/share/[token]` or MCP routes (key = access grant)
- When a new unlisted artifact is published from the web UI, a 30-day share link is auto-created and shown on the success screen — otherwise the publisher would have no way to view it

**Feedback summarization (the LLM feature):**
- Entry point: `src/lib/services/summarize.ts`
- Cache-first: compare `feedback_summaries.feedback_count` against current `COUNT(feedback)`. Return cached if equal. Call Gemini only when missing, stale, or `force_refresh: true`.
- Result stored in `feedback_summaries` as JSONB with `model`, `prompt_version`, `feedback_count`, `generated_at`
- Summary shape: `{ overall_assessment, open_issues[], suggestions[], questions[], approval_count }`

**MCP server** (`/mcp/src/index.ts`): stdio transport, 7 tools. Tools call `/api/mcp/*` with the API key from env. Minimum working set: `list_artifacts`, `get_artifact`, `create_share_link`, `summarize_feedback`. `update_feedback_status` is MCP/API-only — not exposed in the web UI.

## Secret safety

- Never print, commit, or paste real environment variable values
- Use `.env.example` for variable names only — no values
- Do not expose `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY`, or any Vercel tokens in logs, screenshots, commits, or `WRITEUP.md`

## Key env vars

| Var | Used by |
|---|---|
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` | Browser + server |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | Browser |
| `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` | Server only (storage + admin queries) |
| `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY` | `/api/mcp/*` auth check + MCP server config |
| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | `summarize.ts` service |
| `GEMINI_MODEL` | `summarize.ts` service (defaults to `gemini-2.5-flash` if unset) |

## Database

Four tables: `artifacts`, `feedback`, `share_links`, `feedback_summaries`. Migration in `supabase/migrations/001_initial.sql`. Seed data in `supabase/seed.sql` + `supabase/seed-files/` (3 public artifacts, 4–6 feedback entries each — enough for summarization to produce a meaningful digest on first load).

## Constraints to carry forward

- Never generate a signed URL before the access check passes
- Route handlers are thin; all logic goes in `src/lib/services/`
- No logic duplication between `/api/*` and `/api/mcp/*` routes
- Summarization is always cache-first — do not call Gemini if `feedback_count` is unchanged
- Append-only artifacts: no edit or delete endpoints
- Work one phase at a time — do not start the next phase until the current phase is validated, `docs/TRACKER.md` is updated, and changes are committed
- Before broad architectural changes, explain the tradeoff and ask for confirmation

# Artifact Hub — Writeup

> **Status: DRAFT — decision log only. Final writeup to be completed after implementation.**

---

## What I built and why

_[Final writeup: describe the running system, key flows, and product feel. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft decisions:**
- Chose a frictionless demo workspace — reviewers can browse, leave feedback, summarize, share, and publish test artifacts at the deployed URL without any login or key setup. The reviewer experience was the primary UX constraint.
- Prioritized a polished end-to-end artifact lifecycle (publish → browse → detail → feedback → summarize → share) over broad feature count. A complete, working core is worth more than a sprawling system with gaps.
- Skipped NL search to keep the LLM story focused on one feature that directly addresses the stated pain point.

---

## What I chose not to build and why

_[Final writeup: enumerate cuts and rationale. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft decisions:**
- **No user auth or RBAC** — full auth adds 4–6 hours of complexity for a 2-day build. Access control is still demonstrated through the public/unlisted visibility model, private Supabase Storage with signed URLs, expiring share link tokens, and API-key-protected MCP routes. A reviewer can observe the access model working without needing to sign in.
- **No edit or delete of artifacts** — append-only publish keeps the data model and UI simple. Not needed for the evaluation scope.
- **No NL search** — removed in favour of tag/type filtering, which is sufficient for a demo-sized catalog. Keeping one LLM feature sharp is better than two shallow ones.
- **No rate limiting** — demo environment only; noted here so it is not mistaken for an oversight.
- **`update_feedback_status` is MCP/API-only** — changing a feedback item's status (open → resolved → needs_review) is a trusted review-management action. Anonymous public users should not be able to close or re-open issues left by others. Restricting this to API-key-protected MCP routes keeps the trust boundary explicit without needing full auth.

---

## Architecture overview

_[Final writeup: system diagram or prose description. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft notes:**
- Next.js App Router on Vercel — single deployable unit
- Supabase Postgres for four tables: `artifacts`, `feedback`, `share_links`, `feedback_summaries`
- Supabase Storage — private bucket only; all signed URLs generated server-side after access checks
- Visibility model: `public` artifacts appear in the gallery and at `/artifacts/[id]`; `unlisted` artifacts return 403 at that route and are only accessible via `/share/[token]`
- Separated normal web API routes (`/api/*`, open for demo) from protected MCP adapter routes (`/api/mcp/*`, require `x-api-key`). Both call the same `src/lib/services/` functions — no logic duplication.

---

## How the MCP integration works

_[Final writeup: MCP config snippet, tool list, example conversation. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft notes:**
- MCP server is a Node.js stdio process in `/mcp`. Reviewer adds it to Claude Desktop config with `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY` and `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL` env vars.
- MCP tools call `/api/mcp/*` HTTP adapter routes. The API key is the access grant — MCP callers can list and access unlisted artifacts, bypass the visibility check, and perform write actions.
- MCP is on the critical path. Minimum required tools verified before final deploy: `list_artifacts`, `get_artifact`, `create_share_link`, `summarize_feedback`. Then: `publish_artifact`, `add_feedback`, `update_feedback_status`.
- 7 tools total: `list_artifacts`, `get_artifact`, `publish_artifact`, `add_feedback`, `update_feedback_status`, `create_share_link`, `summarize_feedback`.

---

## Where and why I used LLM capabilities

_[Final writeup: describe the summarization feature, prompt design, caching behaviour. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft notes:**
- **Feedback summarization** — chosen because it directly addresses the stated pain point: feedback scattered across Slack threads with no synthesis. On any artifact detail page, "Summarize Feedback" produces a structured digest: overall assessment, open issues, suggestions, questions, approval count.
- Powered by **Gemini** (`@google/genai`, model: `gemini-2.5-flash` by default). Text-only input — no grounding, images, or audio needed.
- Cache-first: result stored in `feedback_summaries` with `feedback_count` at generation time. If current count matches, the cached summary is returned without calling Gemini. Only regenerates when feedback has been added since the last summary, or `force_refresh` is set.
- `model` and `prompt_version` are stored with each summary for transparency in this writeup.
- Feature is immediately testable from seeded artifacts (pre-populated with feedback across all four types).

---

## Deployment approach

_[Final writeup: live URL, env var list, Supabase project details. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft notes:**
- Next.js app deployed to Vercel from this repo
- Supabase hosted project (Postgres + private Storage bucket)
- Required env vars: `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_MODEL`

---

## Demo admin key

_[Fill in after deployment — document the demo API key here for the reviewer's MCP config and direct API testing.]_

---

## What I'd do next with another week

_[Final writeup: honest prioritized list. Fill in after implementation.]_

**Draft ideas:**
- User accounts with artifact ownership (Supabase Auth)
- NL search using pgvector embeddings
- Feedback status management in the web UI (currently MCP/API-only)
- Artifact versioning
- Rate limiting on feedback and publish endpoints

Product Roadmap — Q2 2026

Generated by Claude  ·  Last updated May 2026  ·  Owner: Product Team  ·  Status: Under Review

Objective

Ship the core reviewer experience: structured feedback, AI-powered summarization, and expiring share links. This quarter focuses on closing the feedback loop — getting artifacts in front of the right people and making the resulting conversations actionable without a project management tool.

Feature Backlog

Feature Priority Owner Status Notes
Structured feedback form core High Frontend ✓ Done Approval / suggestion / issue / question types with status tracking
AI feedback summarization AI High Backend ✓ Done Gemini 2.5 Flash, cache-first — only calls LLM when feedback changes
Expiring share links High Backend ✓ Done DB-backed tokens with configurable TTL; 410 on expiry
MCP server (7 tools) AI High Platform ✓ Done stdio transport; Claude Desktop integration; full artifact lifecycle via chat
Public / unlisted visibility High Backend ✓ Done Unlisted artifacts 403 at direct URL; only accessible via share link or MCP key
Dark mode Medium Design In progress Tailwind dark: classes wired; pending design review and QA pass
Email notifications Medium Backend Planned Notify artifact owners when new feedback is left; needs Resend or SES setup
API versioning Medium Platform Planned Version prefix (/api/v1/*) before any external partner integrations
NL search (pgvector) Low Backend Backlog Deferred — tag + type filter is sufficient for demo scale; revisit at 500+ artifacts
Artifact versioning Low Backend Backlog Track revisions; out of scope for append-only v1 model

Success Criteria for Q2

  • Reviewers can leave structured feedback at the deployed URL without creating an account
  • AI summary produces a meaningful digest from 4+ feedback items in under 5 seconds
  • Share links enforce expiry: expired tokens return 410, valid tokens bypass visibility checks
  • All 7 MCP tools verified against deployed URL in Claude Desktop
  • Gallery loads in <1s on a cold Vercel edge request with 3 seeded artifacts

Open Questions

  • Does dark mode proceed independently or wait for the brand refresh to land first?
  • API versioning — add prefix now or wait until we have an external API consumer?
  • Should update_feedback_status be exposed in the web UI for logged-in owners?

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project

Artifact Hub — a platform for publishing, browsing, reviewing, and sharing AI-generated content. Full design: `docs/plans/2026-05-28-artifact-hub-design.md`.

## Execution tracking

`docs/TRACKER.md` is the source of truth for execution state.

- At the start of each phase: read `docs/TRACKER.md`, confirm the current phase and active tasks, and list files you plan to create or modify before touching anything
- During implementation: update `docs/TRACKER.md` only after meaningful milestones, blockers, decisions, or phase completion — not as a running diary
- At the end of each phase: run relevant validation checks, summarize what changed, update `docs/TRACKER.md`, and commit the completed phase

## Commands

```bash
# Web app (Next.js — run from repo root)
npm run dev          # dev server on localhost:3000
npm run build        # production build
npm run lint         # ESLint

# MCP server
cd mcp && npm run build   # compile TypeScript
cd mcp && npm run start   # run stdio server (for testing outside Claude Desktop)

# Database
npx supabase db push
# DESTRUCTIVE — only run after explicit confirmation:
npx supabase db reset --linked   # wipes hosted project and re-seeds
```

## Architecture

Two packages: `src/` (Next.js app, deploys to Vercel) and `mcp/` (Node.js stdio MCP server, run locally by reviewer).

**Request path for all features:**
- Web routes (`/api/*`) — open for demo use, no auth
- MCP adapter routes (`/api/mcp/*`) — require `x-api-key` header, checked in `src/lib/auth.ts`
- Both route groups call the same `src/lib/services/` functions — no logic lives in route handlers

**Storage:** All artifacts in a single private Supabase bucket. Signed URLs are generated server-side in `src/lib/storage.ts` and only after the relevant access check passes. Never expose raw bucket paths or public URLs.

**Visibility enforcement:**
- `public` artifacts: gallery + `/artifacts/[id]`
- `unlisted` artifacts: `/artifacts/[id]` returns 403; only accessible via `/share/[token]` or MCP routes (key = access grant)
- When a new unlisted artifact is published from the web UI, a 30-day share link is auto-created and shown on the success screen — otherwise the publisher would have no way to view it

**Feedback summarization (the LLM feature):**
- Entry point: `src/lib/services/summarize.ts`
- Cache-first: compare `feedback_summaries.feedback_count` against current `COUNT(feedback)`. Return cached if equal. Call Gemini only when missing, stale, or `force_refresh: true`.
- Result stored in `feedback_summaries` as JSONB with `model`, `prompt_version`, `feedback_count`, `generated_at`
- Summary shape: `{ overall_assessment, open_issues[], suggestions[], questions[], approval_count }`

**MCP server** (`/mcp/src/index.ts`): stdio transport, 7 tools. Tools call `/api/mcp/*` with the API key from env. Minimum working set: `list_artifacts`, `get_artifact`, `create_share_link`, `summarize_feedback`. `update_feedback_status` is MCP/API-only — not exposed in the web UI.

## Secret safety

- Never print, commit, or paste real environment variable values
- Use `.env.example` for variable names only — no values
- Do not expose `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY`, or any Vercel tokens in logs, screenshots, commits, or `WRITEUP.md`

## Key env vars

| Var | Used by |
|---|---|
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` | Browser + server |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | Browser |
| `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` | Server only (storage + admin queries) |
| `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY` | `/api/mcp/*` auth check + MCP server config |
| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | `summarize.ts` service |
| `GEMINI_MODEL` | `summarize.ts` service (defaults to `gemini-2.5-flash` if unset) |

## Database

Four tables: `artifacts`, `feedback`, `share_links`, `feedback_summaries`. Migration in `supabase/migrations/001_initial.sql`. Seed data in `supabase/seed.sql` + `supabase/seed-files/` (3 public artifacts, 4–6 feedback entries each — enough for summarization to produce a meaningful digest on first load).

## Constraints to carry forward

- Never generate a signed URL before the access check passes
- Route handlers are thin; all logic goes in `src/lib/services/`
- No logic duplication between `/api/*` and `/api/mcp/*` routes
- Summarization is always cache-first — do not call Gemini if `feedback_count` is unchanged
- Append-only artifacts: no edit or delete endpoints
- Work one phase at a time — do not start the next phase until the current phase is validated, `docs/TRACKER.md` is updated, and changes are committed
- Before broad architectural changes, explain the tradeoff and ask for confirmation

+npm run seed                     # upload seed files + insert 3 artifacts + 15 feedback entries
+npm run seed -- --force          # wipe existing data and re-seed
# DESTRUCTIVE — only run after explicit confirmation:
-npx supabase db reset --linked   # wipes hosted project and re-seeds

input.content:
# Artifact Hub MCP Server

A stdio MCP server that gives Claude Desktop conversational access to the full Artifact Hub artifact lifecycle — publish, browse, review, summarize, and share — without leaving the chat.

## Requirements

- Node.js 18 or later
- A running Artifact Hub deployment (local or Vercel)
- The admin API key (`ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY`) from your `.env.local`

## Setup

```bash
cd mcp
npm install
npm run build          # compiles src/ → dist/
```

The compiled entry point is `mcp/dist/index.js`.

## Claude Desktop Configuration

Add the following to your `claude_desktop_config.json` (found via Claude Desktop → Settings → Developer):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "artifact-hub": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["/absolute/path/to/repo/mcp/dist/index.js"],
      "env": {
        "ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY": "your-admin-key-here",
        "ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL": "https://your-app.vercel.app"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

For local development, use `"ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL": "http://localhost:3000"` and make sure `npm run dev` is running.

Restart Claude Desktop after saving the config. You should see "artifact-hub" appear in the MCP connections list.

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY` | Yes | API key for all `/api/mcp/*` routes. Must match `ARTIFACT_HUB_ADMIN_KEY` in the Next.js app's `.env.local`. |
| `ARTIFACT_HUB_BASE_URL` | Yes | Base URL of the deployed Next.js app. No trailing slash. |

The server writes a warning to stderr (not stdout — stdio transport owns stdout) if either variable is missing.

## Tool Reference

| Tool | Description |
|---|---|
| `list_artifacts` | List artifacts with optional filters: `type` (pdf/image/html), `tags` (array), `visibility` (public/unlisted), `limit`, `offset`. Returns all artifacts including unlisted. |
| `get_artifact` | Full detail for any artifact: metadata, all feedback with status, and a signed 1-hour preview URL. Works for both public and unlisted artifacts. |
| `publish_artifact` | Upload and publish a new artifact from base64-encoded file content. Returns the artifact URL (public) or an auto-created 30-day share link (unlisted). |
| `add_feedback` | Add structured feedback to any artifact. Types: approval, suggestion, issue, question. |
| `update_feedback_status` | Change the status of a feedback item: open, resolved, or needs_review. MCP-only — not available in the web UI. |
| `create_share_link` | Create an expiring share link for any artifact. Default TTL: 30 days. |
| `summarize_feedback` | Get an AI-generated structured digest of all feedback (Gemini 2.5 Flash). Returns cached result if feedback is unchanged. Pass `force_refresh: true` to regenerate. |

## Example Conversation

```
You: List the artifacts in the hub.

Claude: Found 3 artifacts.

1. [HTML] Product Roadmap Q2 2026
   ID: abc-123 | Visibility: public
   Tags: roadmap, product, q2-2026
   Published: May 28, 2026

2. [IMAGE] Artifact Hub Brand Identity
   ...

You: Summarize the feedback on the roadmap.

Claude: Feedback Summary
Generated from 5 feedback items · gemini-2.5-flash

Overall Assessment:
The roadmap is broadly well-received, with two approvals from PM and stakeholder. One blocking issue and two actionable suggestions need resolution before publishing.

Open Issues (1):
• API versioning is missing from the roadmap despite being agreed as Q2 scope.

Suggestions (2):
• Add effort estimates to help the team plan sprints.
• Clarify the relationship between dark mode and the upcoming brand refresh.

Questions (1):
• Can dark mode proceed in parallel with the brand refresh, or is it blocked?

Approvals: 2

You: Mark the API versioning issue as resolved.

Claude: Feedback status updated.
ID: def-456 | Status: resolved
```
